content: Answer in exactly one sentence: are filing deadlines and statutory wording examples of jurisprudence itself or products of it? Pick one and say why. Do not add additional sentences.
Filing deadlines and statutory language are products of jurisprudence, not jurisprudence itself, because they are outputs of the legal system rather than philosophical inquiry into the nature of law.
A clock can tell you precisely what time a filing arrived, but that precision does not make jurisprudence clean; it only shows that a rule was applied.
On the clock: answer in exactly one sentence—when is a broken clock right? Do not add additional sentences.
Apply that answer in exactly one sentence: does occasional correctness of outputs validate the system that produced them, or not? Pick one and say why. Do not add additional sentences.
